decisions.relationship_evidence: Defines a non-parametric multivariate geostatistical data-fusion approach for probabilistic infection-risk mapping from multiple sensor and diagnostic sources.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Validates an epidemic gravity model against global and US H1N1 spread data and specifies conditions under which it is valid.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Implements gravity models to estimate spatial spread and disease risk for two vector-borne infections.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete infectious-disease application of gravity modeling.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops and simulation-tests a spatiotemporal method combining local reproduction-number estimation with a gravity-type spatial-dispersion model.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological development followed by application to Ebola incidence.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Compares adjacency, gravity, radiation, travel-time, and mobile-phone movement models within a poliomyelitis risk model.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Applies and evaluates a gravity-derived effective-distance network approach, identifying outbreak scenarios in which it fails.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit performance and limitation assessment of a gravity-based spread model.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops a spatiotemporal risk-source model using observed population flows to forecast cases and infer geographic spread.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit development of a mobility-informed epidemic-spread model.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses a radiation model for inter-municipality mobility within a spatially structured COVID-19 transmission model.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete COVID-19 application of a radiation mobility model.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Derives and estimates a local gravity model for COVID-19 diffusion and compares power-law and exponential distance-decay forms.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit gravity-model derivation and empirical evaluation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Proposes Deep Gravity, a neural-network model for generating mobility-flow probabilities from geographic features.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit development and multi-region evaluation of a gravity-based mobility model.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses and validates a gravity model to estimate how movement standstills alter livestock trade and disease-risk distribution.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete foreign-animal-disease preparedness application of gravity modeling.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Analyzes observed travel-mobility patterns as regression covariates but does not explicitly use or develop a gravity, radiation, or mobility-flow model.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Not a direct lineage source for the specified model class.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops coupled stochastic within-herd and between-herd models for BVDV spread in a managed metapopulation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Evaluates how infectious-period distribution assumptions affect extinction and epidemic dynamics in metapopulation models.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Compares three assumptions for representing movement networks and evaluates their effects on metapopulation infection dynamics.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit assessment of movement-network assumptions in disease metapopulations.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Formulates a multi-patch SIR model and derives conditions under which asynchronous local pulse-vaccination strategies eradicate infection.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit theoretical development within a metapopulation transmission model.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Benchmarks a structured metapopulation model against an agent-based epidemic model under synchronized assumptions and inputs.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit side-by-side evaluation of metapopulation-model behavior and approximation effects.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Expands a deterministic HME model into connected spatial patches and studies habitat, connectivity, and boundary effects.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete tick-borne-disease metapopulation-model application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses Peruvian data to analyze extinction-recolonization dynamics and CRS risk in epidemic metapopulations.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Evaluates competing metapopulation models of village-level dog-rabies transmission and simulates vaccination effects.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete rabies metapopulation-model application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Proposes a malaria metapopulation model with partial host immunity and derives reproduction-number, stability, and control results.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit mathematical development of a metapopulation transmission model.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses district colonization and persistence models with spatial dependence to analyze H5N1 metapopulation outbreak dynamics.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete avian-influenza metapopulation-dynamics application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Observed cholera persistence is said to suggest a metapopulation pattern, but no metapopulation transmission model is actually used or evaluated.
decisions.lineage_evidence: An empirical observation rather than a direct model lineage source.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Proposes an epidemic model on bipartite metapopulation networks and derives its epidemic threshold analytically and numerically.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit general methodological development of a metapopulation network model.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses patch-occupancy metapopulation models to compare canine-rabies vaccine allocation strategies.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete rabies-control application of metapopulation models.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Advocates integrating metapopulation epidemic modeling into mass-gathering surveillance but does not itself develop, apply, or evaluate such a model.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit conceptual guidance on integrating metapopulation modeling with surveillance and other modeling specialties.
